title: hyoscyamus albus subsp. major
intro: Hyoscyamus albus subsp. major, also known as the large white henbane, is a subspecies of the Hyoscyamus albus plant, belonging to the Solanaceae family and is native to Europe and Western Asia. The medicinal properties of this plant are attributed to its alkaloids, particularly scopolamine and hyoscyamine, which have been used in traditional medicine for their sedative, antispasmodic, and anticholinergic effects. The health benefits of Hyoscyamus albus subsp. major include its ability to alleviate pain, reduce anxiety and insomnia, and treat conditions such as motion sickness and Parkinson's disease. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, and are often used to support the nervous system and promote relaxation.
